english: Juicy Sausage Honey Mustard Hot Dog

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for sausage-filled sweet dough bun with honey mustard sauce; closest parent 'bread roll food product' (FOODON_00002683) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category for this filled sweet-dough sausage bun. No specific filled-bun code exists in FoodEx2 at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 'Bread roll (food)' is the closest parent; no specific code for sausage-filled sweet dough bun with honey mustard sauce.

preparation_method: topped with honey mustard white sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One sausage honey mustard bread roll weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at a Singaporean bakery café.

nutrient_source.title: Frankfurter or hot dog sausage in enriched wheat bun — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite; supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods data for enriched bread roll
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific product. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy entry for 'Frankfurter or hot dog sausage, beef and pork' (FDC ID 172949) with USDA SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, white, commercially prepared, soft' (FDC ID 325871), weighted approximately 35% sausage and 65% bread by mass, then adjusted upward for butter-enriched sweet dough (higher fat and sugar) and honey mustard sauce contribution. This is a medium-confidence composite estimate. Per-100g values reflect the assembled bun as consumed.

gi_source.title: White bread and enriched wheat bun GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific sausage honey mustard sweet dough bun. GI estimated at 65 based on the GI of white bread / enriched wheat bun (GI ~70–75 per University of Sydney GI Database) adjusted downward to approximately 65 to account for fat and protein content from sausage and butter-enriched dough, which slow gastric emptying and reduce glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 43.2) / 100 = 28.1, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 36.0 × 1.2 = 43.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~560 mg per 100 g; ~672 mg per serving) from processed sausage and added salt in dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this bun due to its high sodium load from the processed sausage filling; one serving provides approximately 29% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content and medium-to-high glycemic load (~28 per serving) from enriched white flour dough and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this bun with caution; the high glycemic load of approximately 28 per serving from refined wheat flour and added sugars can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion control or substitution with lower-GI alternatives is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.2 g per 100 g) and sodium from processed sausage and butter-enriched dough, with trans fat present at low levels (~0.2 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this product due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content; regular consumption of processed sausage-containing baked goods is associated with increased LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Sausage Honey Mustard Bread Roll (also marketed as 'Juicy Sausage Honey Mustard Hot Dog') is a Singaporean bakery product sold at RUXU (入续 糖水铺与包), a Chinese-style dessert and bun café at Funan Mall, Singapore. The bun is made using the Japanese sponge dough method (湯種法 or スポンジ法), which produces an exceptionally soft, pillowy, and slightly sweet enriched wheat dough. A whole juicy sausage — typically a pork or chicken frankfurter — is placed on top of or embedded within the dough before baking, giving the product its hot-dog-bun character. After baking, the bun is finished with a homemade honey mustard white sauce, contributing a tangy-sweet flavour contrast to the savoury sausage. The texture is described as soft and plump (鬆軟飽滿). Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, moderate-protein, high-sodium baked good. Per 100 g it provides approximately 285 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 11 g fat (of which 4.2 g saturated), and 36 g carbohydrate with 7.5 g sugar. The sodium content is elevated (~560 mg/100 g) due to the processed sausage. The glycemic load per serving is high (~28), making this a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension should consume sparingly. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten, dairy, egg, or mustard allergies. Best consumed fresh on the day of purchase.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database for this specific product. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data for enriched white bread and frankfurter sausage, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. GI is analogously estimated from white bread GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on direct laboratory analysis of this product.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used for this estimation; vitamin D content of processed sausage and enriched bread is variable and not reliably captured without direct product analysis. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where defensible USDA data was available. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style 'Juicy Sausage Honey Mustard Hot Dog' to the canonical descriptor 'Sausage Honey Mustard Bread Roll' to reflect the actual product format (an enriched baked bun, not a traditional hot dog). The original menu name is preserved in aliases. Cuisine is classified as Chinese-Singaporean Bakery reflecting the restaurant's Chinese dessert-and-bun identity and Singaporean context. Halal status is unconfirmed; RUXU is not listed as a MUIS-certified halal establishment as of the processing date. The description notes the product is best consumed on the day of purchase (truncated in source data). Glycemic load per serving = (65 × 43.2) / 100 = 28.1, where 43.2 g carbohydrate is derived from 36.0 g/100g × 120 g serving size.
